slides.title:
Current state of the science for 
AI-supported systematic review

items.text: TERA (“The Evidence Review Accelerator”): Glasziou group
Speeds up conventional review (“2-week systematic review”)

Tools for scoping, protocol-writing, database searching, deduplication, title/abstract screening, data extraction, and documenting the workflow and results of risk-of-bias tool application (but the judgements are still made by humans)
Does a PRISMA-compliant systematic review of RCTs, more quickly!

items.text: Problems that require new data (e.g. from scientific inquiry):

nodes.text: "Technical" question:  Can we use AI to help with this?
Empirical question:  What is the efficacy of face masks in reducing transmission of respiratory infections?
Methodological question:  How can we improve the narrative [hermeneutic] synthesis of complex [overwhelming] evidence?

MINS was inspired by the overwhelmingness of the primary evidence on face
mask efficacy

Emergent interactions between variables and context

nodes.text: In review logic: from ‘extract and aggregate’ to ‘explain and clarify’
In how we use AI: from ‘process accelerator’ to ‘conversational agent’
In how we value evidence: from ‘hierarchy’ to ‘diversity’
In the tools we use:  from ‘risk-of- bias’  to  ‘mechanisms-of- bias’

content.description: A detailed statistical analysis document showing grid cell results for bias analysis. It presents PR_true values computed across a 27-cell grid (combining 3r × 3p × 3f combinations), with summary statistics including a range of 0.602–0.856 and a median of 0.755. The document includes sections on explicit numeric bounds, interpretation of mechanistic implications, and robustness to plausible bias, concluding that the published adjusted odds ratio is compatible with a real protective effect.

nodes.text: AI: How would ChatGPT 5.2 perform against other similar LLMs?
TRANSFERABILITY: Could this method be applied to other review topics? How to adapt it?
THE NAÏVE USER: Will AI-supported hermeneutic review be unsafe in inexperienced hands?
Next-gen AI: Could agentic AI such as Antigravity take performance to next level?
REPLICABILITY: Would another review team produce the same or equivalent result?
